info.title: VExim Web UI Domain Management API
info.description: API for managing VExim domains with Sanctum token-based authentication. All requests require a valid Bearer token.

bearerAuth.description:
Enter your Sanctum token in the format: Bearer {token}

Tokens are generated locally by Sanctum. Example: Bearer 1|abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz...

type.description: Domain type: local (mail handled locally) or alias (forward to primary domain)

primary_domain_id.description: Required for alias domains. Primary domain ID or name

max_accounts.description: Maximum number of email accounts (null = unlimited)

maxmsgsize.description: Maximum message size in bytes (0 = system default)

sa_tag.description: Spam tag score (required if spamassassin is true)

sa_refuse.description: Spam refuse score (required if spamassassin is true)

admin_user_id.description: User ID or email to assign as initial administrator

user.description: User ID or email to assign
User ID or email to unassign

message.example: Token does not have required ability: domains:create

Unauthorized.description: 401 Unauthorized - No valid token provided or token expired

Forbidden.description: 403 Forbidden - Token does not have the required ability for this endpoint

example.message: Token does not have required ability: domains:create

NotFound.description: 404 Not Found - The requested resource does not exist

get.description:
Get the authenticated user's information, roles, and token abilities from the Bearer token.

**Required Token Ability:** None (just needs valid token)

Get a paginated list of domains. System admins see all domains, domain admins see only their assigned domains.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:view`

200.description: Successful response with list of domains

post.description:
Create a new domain. Only system administrators can create domains.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:create`

get.description:
Search for domains with advanced filters. System admins search all domains, domain admins search only their domains.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:view`

Get current usage statistics and limits for the authenticated domain administrator.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:view`

**Note:** Only available to domain administrators.

Get detailed information about a specific domain including statistics and aliases.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:view`

put.description:
Update domain settings. System admins can update any domain, domain admins can only update domains they manage.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:update`

400.description: Bad request - No data to update

delete.description:
Delete a domain and all associated data. Only system administrators can delete domains.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:delete`

patch.description:
Partially update domain settings. Similar to PUT but only sends changed fields.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:update`

400.description: Bad request - No data to update

post.description:
Activate a disabled domain. System admins can activate any domain, domain admins can only activate domains they manage.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:update`

Deactivate an active domain. System admins can deactivate any domain, domain admins can only deactivate domains they manage.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:update`

get.description:
Get detailed usage statistics for a domain including user counts, quota usage, and account availability.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:statistics`

Get all users assigned to a domain with their roles. Only domain admins and system admins can view this.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:users`

post.description:
Assign a user to a domain with a specific role. System admins can assign any user, domain admins can only assign users to domains they manage.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:assign`

message.example: User 'john@example.com' has been assigned as Administrator to domain 'example.com' successfully!
User 'john@example.com' role updated to 'domain_admin' for domain 'example.com'

post.description:
Remove a user's assignment from a domain. Prevents removing the last domain administrator.

**Required Token Ability:** `domains:unassign`

message.example: User 'john@example.com' has been unassigned from domain 'example.com' successfully!
